exec_args.sql: SELECT user_id FROM user WHERE strftime('%Y', yelping_since) = '2016';

args.query: SELECT user_id FROM user WHERE strftime('%Y', CAST(yelping_since AS DATE)) = '2016';

args.query: SELECT r.business_ref, COUNT(*) AS review_count FROM review r JOIN (SELECT user_id FROM user WHERE strftime('%Y', CAST(SUBSTRING(yelping_since, 8, 4) || '-' || CASE SUBSTRING(yelping_since, 4, 3) WHEN 'Jan' THEN '01' WHEN 'Feb' THEN '02' WHEN 'Mar' THEN '03' WHEN 'Apr' THEN '04' WHEN 'May' THEN '05' WHEN 'Jun' THEN '06' WHEN 'Jul' THEN '07' WHEN 'Aug' THEN '08' WHEN 'Sep' THEN '09' WHEN 'Oct' THEN '10' WHEN 'Nov' THEN '11' WHEN 'Dec' THEN '12' END || '-' || LPAD(SUBSTRING(yelping_since, 1, 2), 2, '0') AS DATE)) = '2016') u ON r.user_id = u.user_id WHERE CAST(SUBSTRING(r.date, 1, 4) AS INT) >= 2016 GROUP BY r.business_ref;

exec_args.sql: SELECT r.business_ref, COUNT(*) AS review_count FROM review r JOIN (SELECT user_id FROM user WHERE strftime('%Y', CAST(SUBSTRING(yelping_since, 8, 4) || '-' || CASE SUBSTRING(yelping_since, 4, 3) WHEN 'Jan' THEN '01' WHEN 'Feb' THEN '02' WHEN 'Mar' THEN '03' WHEN 'Apr' THEN '04' WHEN 'May' THEN '05' WHEN 'Jun' THEN '06' WHEN 'Jul' THEN '07' WHEN 'Aug' THEN '08' WHEN 'Sep' THEN '09' WHEN 'Oct' THEN '10' WHEN 'Nov' THEN '11' WHEN 'Dec' THEN '12' END || '-' || LPAD(SUBSTRING(yelping_since, 1, 2), 2, '0') AS DATE)) = '2016') u ON r.user_id = u.user_id WHERE CAST(SUBSTRING(r.date, 1, 4) AS INT) >= 2016 GROUP BY r.business_ref;

args.query: SELECT r.business_ref, COUNT(*) AS review_count FROM review r JOIN (SELECT user_id FROM user WHERE strftime('%Y', CAST(SUBSTRING(yelping_since, 8, 4) || '-' || CASE SUBSTRING(yelping_since, 4, 3) WHEN 'Jan' THEN '01' WHEN 'Feb' THEN '02' WHEN 'Mar' THEN '03' WHEN 'Apr' THEN '04' WHEN 'May' THEN '05' WHEN 'Jun' THEN '06' WHEN 'Jul' THEN '07' WHEN 'Aug' THEN '08' WHEN 'Sep' THEN '09' WHEN 'Oct' THEN '10' WHEN 'Nov' THEN '11' WHEN 'Dec' THEN '12' END || '-' || LPAD(SUBSTRING(yelping_since, 1, 2), 2, '0') AS DATE)) = '2016') u ON r.user_id = u.user_id WHERE r.date LIKE '2016-%' OR r.date LIKE '2017-%' OR r.date LIKE '2018-%' OR r.date LIKE '2019-%' OR r.date LIKE '2020-%' OR r.date LIKE '2021-%' OR r.date LIKE '2022-%' OR r.date LIKE '2023-%' OR r.date LIKE '2024-%' GROUP BY r.business_ref;

exec_args.sql: SELECT r.business_ref, COUNT(*) AS review_count FROM review r JOIN (SELECT user_id FROM user WHERE strftime('%Y', CAST(SUBSTRING(yelping_since, 8, 4) || '-' || CASE SUBSTRING(yelping_since, 4, 3) WHEN 'Jan' THEN '01' WHEN 'Feb' THEN '02' WHEN 'Mar' THEN '03' WHEN 'Apr' THEN '04' WHEN 'May' THEN '05' WHEN 'Jun' THEN '06' WHEN 'Jul' THEN '07' WHEN 'Aug' THEN '08' WHEN 'Sep' THEN '09' WHEN 'Oct' THEN '10' WHEN 'Nov' THEN '11' WHEN 'Dec' THEN '12' END || '-' || LPAD(SUBSTRING(yelping_since, 1, 2), 2, '0') AS DATE)) = '2016') u ON r.user_id = u.user_id WHERE r.date LIKE '2016-%' OR r.date LIKE '2017-%' OR r.date LIKE '2018-%' OR r.date LIKE '2019-%' OR r.date LIKE '2020-%' OR r.date LIKE '2021-%' OR r.date LIKE '2022-%' OR r.date LIKE '2023-%' OR r.date LIKE '2024-%' GROUP BY r.business_ref;

Without a categories field or another way to map each business to categories, I cannot aggregate review counts by category.\n\nIf you have an updated schema or another table/field that lists categories for each business, I could use that to compute the requested top 5 categories."
